principles: Motion is part of the brand voice: it sets tone, rhythm, and personality on top-of-funnel pages.
Expressiveness is earned by context — marketing and onboarding canvases, never dense operational tooling.
Choreograph sequences with stagger and easing so attention flows in a deliberate reading order.
Performance and accessibility are non-negotiable: 60fps transform/opacity only, and a full reduced-motion path.
Never trap or hijack the user; scroll, focus, and navigation remain under their control at all times.
entrance: Sections reveal on scroll with a 24px translate, 0.96 to 1.0 scale and fade over base (360ms), staggered ~60ms per child using the decelerate curve.
exit: Elements leave with a fade and 16px translate over fast (200ms) accelerate; full-screen overlays scale down slightly as they dismiss.
spatial_navigation: Page and route transitions use a slow (600ms) layered crossfade with parallax depth between foreground and background; direction reflects nav intent but never blocks input.
list_changes: Galleries and feature grids animate items in with a staggered scale-and-fade; reordering uses FLIP with the decelerate curve over base (360ms).
status_change: Marketing status (e.g. plan selected, form accepted) gets an expressive but single-shot flourish — a check draw or colour sweep over base (360ms) — never a repeating loop.
loading: Hero and media use a graceful blur-up / fade-in from placeholder over base (360ms); long operations show a branded determinate indicator, not a fake animated bar.
errors: Form errors fade in with the inline message and a single soft shake (one cycle, ~180ms); colour and text always carry the meaning so the shake is pure emphasis.
decorative_motion: Permitted and intentional: gradient drifts, parallax, hover micro-interactions and looping ambient accents — but only in hero/marketing zones, paused when offscreen, and capped so they never compete with a primary CTA.
reduced_motion: All scroll-reveals, parallax, stagger, blur-up, drift, and shake are disabled. Content renders in its final position and full opacity immediately; any loop becomes a static frame and transitions become instant or a single fast (200ms) fade.
